is an American actress and writer. On television, she is known for her roles in the [USA Network](/wiki/USA_Network "USA Network") series *[The Sinner](/wiki/The_Sinner_(TV_series) "The Sinner (TV series)")* (2021) and the [Disney+](/wiki/Disney%2B "Disney+") series *[Renegade Nell](/wiki/Renegade_Nell "Renegade Nell")* (2024). Her films include *[Campus Code](/wiki/Campus_Code "Campus Code")* (2015) and *[The Trial of the Chicago 7](/wiki/The_Trial_of_the_Chicago_7 "The Trial of the Chicago 7")* (2020).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American actress

| Alice Kremelberg | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | [Long Island, New York](/wiki/Long_Island,_New_York "Long Island, New York"), U.S. |
| Alma mater | [Fordham University](/wiki/Fordham_University "Fordham University") |
| Occupation | Actress |
| Years active | 2006–present |

**Alice Kremelberg** is an American actress and writer. On television, she is known for her roles in the [USA Network](/wiki/USA_Network "USA Network") series *[The Sinner](/wiki/The_Sinner_(TV_series) "The Sinner (TV series)")* (2021) and the [Disney+](/wiki/Disney%2B "Disney+") series *[Renegade Nell](/wiki/Renegade_Nell "Renegade Nell")* (2024). Her films include *[Campus Code](/wiki/Campus_Code "Campus Code")* (2015) and *[The Trial of the Chicago 7](/wiki/The_Trial_of_the_Chicago_7 "The Trial of the Chicago 7")* (2020).

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alice_Kremelberg&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Kremelberg was born and raised on [Long Island, New York](/wiki/Long_Island,_New_York "Long Island, New York").[[1]](#cite_note-1) She attended the [Professional Performing Arts School](/wiki/Professional_Performing_Arts_School "Professional Performing Arts School") in [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan") for high school before going on to study at [Fordham University](/wiki/Fordham_University "Fordham University"), the [Atlantic Acting School](/wiki/Atlantic_Theater_Company "Atlantic Theater Company")'s Conservatory,[[2]](#cite_note-2) and with the [Upright Citizens Brigade](/wiki/Upright_Citizens_Brigade "Upright Citizens Brigade").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alice_Kremelberg&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

While still in high school, Kremelberg made her television debut in 2006 with roles in an episode of *[Law & Order: Special Victims Unit](/wiki/Law_%26_Order:_Special_Victims_Unit "Law & Order: Special Victims Unit")* on [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC") and as a young version of [Crystal Chappell](/wiki/Crystal_Chappell "Crystal Chappell")'s character [Olivia Spencer](/wiki/Olivia_Spencer "Olivia Spencer") in the [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") soap opera *[Guiding Light](/wiki/Guiding_Light "Guiding Light")*.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Kremelberg guest starred in the 2010 *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")* special episode "[Unearthed](/wiki/Unearthed_(Fringe) "Unearthed (Fringe)")" on [Fox](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company"). She had her first named film role in the 2012 drama *[Nancy, Please](/wiki/Nancy,_Please "Nancy, Please")*. In 2013, she played Reese in *[The Michael J. Fox Show](/wiki/The_Michael_J._Fox_Show "The Michael J. Fox Show")* on [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC"). She had her first starring film role as Izzy in the 2015 science fiction film *[Campus Code](/wiki/Campus_Code "Campus Code")*. She then played Sorrell in [Sonja O'Hara](/wiki/Sonja_O%27Hara "Sonja O'Hara")'s [Shudder](/wiki/Shudder_(streaming_service) "Shudder (streaming service)") series *Doomsday*.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

In 2018 and 2019, Kremelberg had a recurring role as Nicole Eckelcamp in the fifth and sixth seasons of the [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix") series *[Orange is the New Black](/wiki/Orange_is_the_New_Black "Orange is the New Black")*.[[6]](#cite_note-6) She received a [Daytime Emmy Award](/wiki/Daytime_Emmy_Award "Daytime Emmy Award") nomination for her performance in the web series *The Feels*, on which she also has writing credits. She wrote and produced the short film *O, Ryan*.

Kremelberg portrayed [Bernardine Dohrn](/wiki/Bernardine_Dohrn "Bernardine Dohrn") in [Aaron Sorkin](/wiki/Aaron_Sorkin "Aaron Sorkin")'s 2020 film *[The Trial of the Chicago 7](/wiki/The_Trial_of_the_Chicago_7 "The Trial of the Chicago 7")* [[7]](#cite_note-7) Also in 2020, she guest starred in an installment of the [Hulu](/wiki/Hulu "Hulu") horror anthology *[Monsterland](/wiki/Monsterland_(TV_series) "Monsterland (TV series)")*. Kremelberg joined the main cast of the [USA Network](/wiki/USA_Network "USA Network") series *[The Sinner](/wiki/The_Sinner_(TV_series) "The Sinner (TV series)")* as Percy Muldoon for its fourth and final season, which aired in 2021.[[8]](#cite_note-8) She also played a young version of [Caroline Williams](/wiki/Caroline_Williams "Caroline Williams")' character in the film *Ten Minutes to Midnight*.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Kremelberg stars as Sofia Wilmot in the British [Disney+](/wiki/Disney%2B "Disney+") series *[Renegade Nell](/wiki/Renegade_Nell "Renegade Nell")*.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alice_Kremelberg&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life")]

As of November 2021,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Alice_Kremelberg&action=edit) Kremelberg is openly queer and uses she/they pronouns. [[11]](#cite_note-11) She splits her time between New York, Los Angeles, and London.[[12]](#cite_note-12)
